evidence_text:

and self-switchable ring-opening polymerization (ROP) of three
different cyclic ester monomers (lactide (LA), rac-β-butyrolactone

neously exhibit high optical transparency, mechanical robustness, and excellent
sustainability remains a formidable challenge. Herein, we report a conjugation-
disruption strategy to fabricate colorless and chemically recyclable HRIPs with
highmechanicalrobustnessandefficientchemicalrecyclability,denotedasPTU-4.

due to the presence of interconnected amorphous and crystalline regions.
Extended or folded chain crystals, along with entanglements and tie
molecules, are formed depending on the chain length. The material’s
structure determines its final performance, so understanding the impact of
chain length is essential. Broadband dielectric spectroscopy is highly
sensitive to the molecular dynamics of polar polymers. In this work, a

physical properties and have been attracted considerable attention from both scientific respect and technology
respect. Piezoelectricity is one of the most important properties of ferroelectrics, which has been widely used in

title: How to Characterize Supramolecular Polymers: A User Guide
evidence_text:
Unlike traditional macromolecules, supramolecular poly-
mers are assemblies composed of individual monomeric units that are
connected not by covalent bonds but through reversible, noncovalent
interactions. This fundamental distinction from conventional polymers
endows them with a wide range of novel and tunable properties, such as
intrinsic dynamic reversibility, responsiveness to external stimuli (e.g.,
temperature,pH,light,andchemicalenvironment),self-healingcapability,
and the ability to unde

title: Architecture Effect on Network Phase Formation from Controlled
evidence_text:
This work aims to examine the architecture effect
on the self-assembly of high-χ block copolymers (BCPs),
polystyrene-block-polydimethylsiloxane (PS-b-PDMS). Lamellae-
forming diblock and star BCPs with three and six arms were
synthesized for self-assembly. A variety of self-assembled phases,
especially network phases, can be formed by tuning the solvent
evaporation rate for solution casting using PS-selective solvents
(referred to as controlled self-assembly). In contrast to diblocks
withlinear
original_title: Architecture Effect on Network Phase Formation from Controlled

title: Flaw-Tolerant Fracture Enabled by Liquid-like
evidence_text:
Polydomain liquid crystal elastomers (P-LCEs) with randomly oriented directors exhibit
extraordinary in-plane liquid-like mechanical properties, attributed to the minimal energy cost of local nematic
director rotation, distinguishing them from conventional elastomers. This study reveals that during tensile tearing,
notched P-LCEs demonstrate exceptional strain homogenization across the specimen, effectively mitigating the
crack-tip strain concentration and suppressing crack-tip opening over a br

design of elastomeric biomaterials that must simultaneously achieve high strength, large
deformability, and long-term durability. Here, we report a polyurethane elastomer constructed
via a multiscale energy-dissipation strategy that establishes a direct structure-fatigue-property
relationship. By integrating molecular-scale chemical cross-linking, microscale physical cross-

of wormlike micellar solutions using dissipative particle dynamics
simulations. As the extension rate increases, the steady-state
extensional viscosity initially increases and subsequently decreases
after reaching a maximum, as observed in experiments. We reveal

mechanical properties, restricting ourselves to soft networks
made of flexible polymers and displaying entropic elasticity.

inversed one. In this work, however, thermoplastic elastomers based on two thermodynamically immiscible components,
poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) and poly(propylene glycol) (PPG), exhibit speed-induced extensibility (SIE). This leads to

title: Correlation between Structural Flexibility of H‑Bonding Motifs and
evidence_text:
Hydrogen bonds (H-bonds) are effective dynamic cross-links that
endow polymers with dynamic mechanical functions such as toughness, self-
recoverability, and self-healing ability. While conventional complementary
multidentate H-bonding groups are advantageous for enhancing binding strength
due to their rigid structures, flexible multidentate H-bonding groups such as
aliphatic diols have recently been found to achieve a good balance between
dynamicity and mechanical robustness. In this study, we

title: 第 38卷 第 2 期
evidence_text:
Associative polymers (APs), due to their unique dynamic behaviors, show significant potential
in fields such as self-healing, shape memory, and rheological modification. The presence of associative
interactions leads to a coupling between strand motion and associative reactions, which not only broadens
the application of APs, but also adds complexity to their processing. Developing a rheological theory for
APs is essential for understanding their viscoelastic properties at the molecular level. S

title: Design of a Recyclable Thiol−ene Network with Dynamic Ether
evidence_text:
Thiol−ene click chemistry has been widely used for
synthesizing cross-linked polymers, but conventional networks
contain irreversible thioether bonds, preventing reprocessing and
contributing to waste. To address this, dynamic covalent linkages
have been introduced. However, designing chemistry that permits
the dynamicity and robustness of the network is a formidable
challenge. Besides, regulating dynamicity and creep in the network
bearing the dynamic linkages remains another major challenge. T
